checklist_scores.label: Find the canonical repository home URL.
checklist_scores.note: Canonical home is `https://pyxida.aueb.gr/`; HTML head reports `Generator: DSpace 7.6.2-SNAPSHOT`.

checklist_scores.label: List the collections or subcollections available on the website.
checklist_scores.note: Top-level communities are `Ακαδημαϊκό Αποθετήριο`, `Ιστορικό Αρχείο ΟΠΑ`, and `Ψηφιακές Συλλογές Οικονομικών και Κοινωνικών Επιστημών`. Community/collection summaries were captured from `core/communities` and per-community `.../collections` endpoints.

checklist_scores.label: Show how to enter the relevant collection from the repository home.
checklist_scores.note: Home SSR calls `core/communities/search/top?page=0&size=5`; from there the path is Home -> `Ακαδημαϊκό Αποθετήριο` -> department community -> `Μεταπτυχιακές Εργασίες` collection. Handle routes also redirect directly to canonical UUID pages.

checklist_scores.label: Describe the page levels from repo home to collection to list to item to PDF.
checklist_scores.note: Verified home (`/`), community (`/communities/{uuid}`), collection (`/collections/{uuid}`), item (`/items/{uuid}` via handle redirect), and PDF download (`/bitstreams/{uuid}/download` -> `/server/api/core/bitstreams/{uuid}/content`).

checklist_scores.label: Identify the platform or architecture family.
checklist_scores.note: DSpace 7 Angular SSR front-end with `/server/api` HAL REST API, OAI-PMH at `/server/oai/request`, and signposting endpoints.

checklist_scores.label: Identify any public APIs or machine-readable endpoints for metadata or file discovery.
checklist_scores.note: Confirmed `/server/api` HAL REST routes, OAI-PMH at `/server/oai/request` (`oai_identify.xml`), signposting linkset JSON (`sample_item_linkset.json`), and DataCite `describedby` links on item pages.

checklist_scores.label: Find any claim about total files overall and per collection, and record how site claims compare to API-reported counts.
checklist_scores.note: Current direct probes show 12,265 items via `discover_global_count.json`. Collection/community counts such as 9,965 for `Ακαδημαϊκό Αποθετήριο`, 1,483 for collection `0981f406...`, 1,481, 1,467, 1,294, and 1,228 matched between `communities_top.json`, `collections_page0.json`, `discover_scope_0981f406_page0.json`, and preserved community summaries.

checklist_scores.label: Identify which metadata/API routes and file-download routes should be benchmarked for real throughput.
checklist_scores.note: Benchmarked `server/api/discover/search/objects?...scope=...` for metadata and `/bitstreams/{uuid}/download` -> `/server/api/core/bitstreams/{uuid}/content` for files. `sample_pdf_head_headers.txt` and `slow_file_probe_req3.headers.txt` show that the front-end route redirects normally until the backend/content path starts 429ing.

checklist_scores.label: List the dominant content types and low-priority content.
checklist_scores.note: Born-digital theses dominate value and volume. Historical scanned books, yearbooks, and issue-level journal collections are meaningful but OCR-weak. Auxiliaries (`TEXT`, `THUMBNAIL`, license files) and the empty photo archive are low priority.

checklist_scores.label: Define the subcollections worth crawling first.
checklist_scores.note: Crawl the eight departmental `Μεταπτυχιακές Εργασίες` collections first, then doctoral collections, then optionally the stronger Greek scanned-book collections (`Σπάνιες εκδόσεις`, `Παλαιά Συγγράμματα`) if OCR quality is acceptable.

checklist_scores.label: Probe request tolerance conservatively and record what metadata/file request frequency appears safe.
checklist_scores.note: Metadata tolerated 6 sequential requests at about 2.28 rps with no 429. File delivery returned 200 for PDFs at 2026-03-29T15:14:59Z and 2026-03-29T15:15:09Z, then 429 on the third download at 2026-03-29T15:15:20Z despite a 10-second gap; a later retry at 2026-03-29T15:15:59Z still 429ed. No safe sustained file cadence meeting the 48-hour target was established.

checklist_scores.label: Assess Greek fit, content quality, and extraction ease.
checklist_scores.note: Greek fit is high at repository level and strongest in legacy theses and scanned Greek collections. Content quality and extraction ease are highest for born-digital theses; scanned historical branches are lower-ease follow-ons.

issue_labels.label: count_completeness
issue_labels.note: Observed coverage relative to reported counts.

issue_labels.label: eta_health
issue_labels.note: Download ETA health versus the 48-hour target.

issue_labels.label: quality_fit
issue_labels.note: Greek fit, content quality, and extraction ease.

issue_labels.label: overall_progress
issue_labels.note: Weighted summary of current stage health.

improvement.decision_reason: Discover is already strong on scope and counts at 96.68% overall progress with 12,265.0 claimed and 12,265.0 observed items, but request capacity is still partial: metadata tolerated 2.2798073387712776 rps, bitstream delivery fell to 628991.5531151413 bytes/second, and the estimated full-download ETA is 52.335209739960526 hours versus the 48.0-hour target. File probing returned 200 at 2026-03-29T15:14:59Z and 2026-03-29T15:15:09Z, then 429 on the third PDF at 2026-03-29T15:15:20Z despite a 10-second gap, with another 429 at 2026-03-29T15:15:59Z. Reviewer recovery stats show 1 discover failure:partial case with 1 paired case, 0 improved cases, avg_end_progress_percent 96.68, and 0 promotable_next_cases, so there is no evidence that another routine retry will clear the remaining gap without a user-approved tradeoff.
improvement_hypotheses: A longer backend cooldown than the visible front-end reset window is governing bitstream delivery, so a sparse timed probe could still establish a safe sustained cadence.

Separating metadata enumeration from file delivery, using REST or OAI-PMH for metadata and a single throttled file lane for PDFs, is the most plausible way to preserve completeness without increasing 429 risk.

If the user prefers time-bounded value over whole-repository completeness, the eight departmental masters-thesis collections plus the doctoral lane (10,392 items total) are the best Greek-text-first scope reduction.
changes_to_try: If full coverage is still required, run a longer low-frequency file probe that spaces requests well beyond 10 seconds, records reset headers for every attempt, and recomputes ETA from the observed sustainable cadence before starting download work.

Keep metadata discovery on the proven machine-readable surfaces and split it from file delivery so the file lane can stay at one conservative stream with strict 429 backoff and exact throughput logging.

If the user wants a faster path, narrow the first crawl to the 10,029-item masters-thesis collections plus 363 doctoral items and defer scan-heavy collections such as Σπάνιες εκδόσεις and Παλαιά Συγγράμματα.
improvement.user_decision_question: Should the next step be a slower-than-target full crawl at the current one-stream boundary, a longer cooldown-mapping probe to try to recover a sub-48-hour path, or a narrowed high-value crawl focused on the 10,392 thesis/dissertation items?

checklist_scores.note: Benchmarked `server/api/discover/search/objects?...scope=...` for metadata and `/bitstreams/{uuid}/download` -> `/server/api/core/bitstreams/{uuid}/content` for files. `sample_pdf_head_headers.txt`, `slow_file_probe_req3.headers.txt`, and the new `file_probe_30_30_attempt3.headers.txt` all show the same pattern: the front-end redirect stays 302/200 while the backend content route eventually emits HTTP 429.

checklist_scores.label: List the dominant content types and low-priority content.
checklist_scores.note: Born-digital theses dominate value and volume. Historical scanned books, yearbooks, and issue-level journal collections are meaningful but OCR-weak. Auxiliaries (`TEXT`, `THUMBNAIL`, license files) and the empty photo archive are low priority.

checklist_scores.label: Probe request tolerance conservatively and record what metadata/file request frequency appears safe.
checklist_scores.note: Metadata still tolerated 6 sequential requests at about 2.28 rps with no 429. Full-PDF delivery succeeded twice and then 429ed on the third request with 20-second gaps (200 at 2026-03-29T15:50:19Z and 15:50:40Z; 429 at 15:51:01Z) and again with 30-second gaps after a 447-second quiet period (200 at 2026-03-29T15:58:29Z and 15:59:00Z; 429 at 15:59:31Z). No sustained no-error file cadence was established.

checklist_scores.label: Assess Greek fit, content quality, and extraction ease.
checklist_scores.note: Greek fit is high at repository level and strongest in legacy theses and scanned Greek collections. Content quality and extraction ease are highest for born-digital theses; scanned historical branches are lower-ease follow-ons.

issue_labels.label: count_completeness
issue_labels.note: Observed coverage relative to reported counts.

issue_labels.label: eta_health
issue_labels.note: Download ETA health versus the 48-hour target.

issue_labels.label: quality_fit
issue_labels.note: Greek fit, content quality, and extraction ease.

issue_labels.label: overall_progress
issue_labels.note: Weighted summary of current stage health.

improvement.decision_reason: Discover remains strong on repository shape and Greek-text fit (`overall_progress_percent` 87.39, `stage_completion_percent` 95.83, `count_completeness_percent` 100.0, `quality_fit_percent` 100.0) with 12,265.0 claimed and 12,265.0 observed items, but `request_capacity` is still unresolved: metadata tolerated 6 requests in 2.6318013359996257 seconds (2.2798073387712776 rps), while the file lane returned 200, 200, then 429 at 20-second gaps (2026-03-29T15:50:19Z, 15:50:40Z, 15:51:01Z) and again 200, 200, then 429 at 30-second gaps after a 447.407767-second quiet period (2026-03-29T15:58:29Z, 15:59:00Z, 15:59:31Z). The conservative failed-window boundary is 115.70500708805021 files/hour and `estimated_eta_hours` 106.0023270269235 for 12,265 items versus the 48.0-hour target, so the next move is a user tradeoff rather than a routine retry or an advance. Recovery stats also show 1 discover `failure:partial` case with 1 paired case, 0 improved cases, avg_end_progress_percent 96.68, and 0 promotable_next_cases, which does not support another automatic retry.
improvement_hypotheses: Pyxida's backend bitstream throttle is stricter than the visible front-end rate-limit headers, so a slower single-stream cooldown probe may improve confidence but has not yet shown a sub-48-hour path.
Separating metadata collection from file delivery remains correct for this DSpace 7 lane because metadata surfaces are already stable while file delivery alone is triggering 429s.
Without a materially faster safe file cadence or a user-approved strategic tradeoff, full direct download of the 12,265-item repository is not on track to finish inside 48 hours.
changes_to_try: If the user wants more certainty, run one longer single-stream file probe at roughly 45-90 second spacing with full timestamp and header logging until either at least 4-6 consecutive original-PDF downloads succeed or the cooldown boundary is measured clearly.

Keep metadata on `/server/api/discover/search/objects` and other public machine-readable surfaces, and keep file delivery at parallelism 1 with strict 429 backoff while the backend limit is unresolved.
If the user does not want to spend more probe time on a likely over-48-hour lane, stop discover retries here and treat Pyxida as metadata-complete but file-capacity-partial.
improvement.user_decision_question: Do you want to authorize a longer 45-90 second single-stream cooldown probe to map Pyxida's real PDF limit, knowing the current conservative boundary is 115.70500708805021 files/hour and 106.0023270269235 hours for 12,265 items, or should Pyxida be accepted as metadata-complete but file-capacity-partial?

final_reason: Discover remains strong on repository shape and Greek-text fit (`overall_progress_percent` 87.39, `stage_completion_percent` 95.83, `count_completeness_percent` 100.0, `quality_fit_percent` 100.0) with 12,265.0 claimed and 12,265.0 observed items, but `request_capacity` is still unresolved: metadata tolerated 6 requests in 2.6318013359996257 seconds (2.2798073387712776 rps), while the file lane returned 200, 200, then 429 at 20-second gaps (2026-03-29T15:50:19Z, 15:50:40Z, 15:51:01Z) and again 200, 200, then 429 at 30-second gaps after a 447.407767-second quiet period (2026-03-29T15:58:29Z, 15:59:00Z, 15:59:31Z). The conservative failed-window boundary is 115.70500708805021 files/hour and `estimated_eta_hours` 106.0023270269235 for 12,265 items versus the 48.0-hour target, so the next move is a user tradeoff rather than a routine retry or an advance. Recovery stats also show 1 discover `failure:partial` case with 1 paired case, 0 improved cases, avg_end_progress_percent 96.68, and 0 promotable_next_cases, which does not support another automatic retry.
